project.name: Hostile "Fixture" & <Sons>, Ltd. `v0`
project.goal:
A "goal" with 'single quotes',
an escaped newline, {{lowercase braces}}, `backticks`, & ampersands — plus a trailing backslash \ and a $dollar.
project.primary_user: A user who types "quotes" & <angle brackets> into every form field they meet.
project.core_problem:
Systems break when text contains {{curly}} braces, `ticks`, "quotes",
or newlines; this fixture proves the renderer does not.
project.core_journey:
1. Feed the renderer text full of "quotes" & braces.
2. Render.
3. Every structured file still parses; every prose file carries the text verbatim.
project.success_measure: All rendered JSON/TOML files parse; the hostile strings land verbatim in Markdown, escaped in structured files.
project.success_metrics:
- structured-file parse failures -- zero
- hostile characters altered in prose -- zero
project.riskiest_assumption: Plain per-format escaping ({{braces}} included) is enough — no template engine needed.
project.req_ac_list:
- [ ] **REQ-AC1:** `pyproject.toml` parses with the hostile description "as-is" (escaped).
- [ ] **REQ-AC2:** `.devcontainer/devcontainer.json` parses with the hostile name & ampersand.
- [ ] **REQ-AC3:** Markdown docs carry quotes, `backticks`, {{braces}} and newlines verbatim.
project.non_goals:
- No attempt to sanitize or prettify hostile text; it must land verbatim.
- No support for text containing HTML comment markers (validated out).
project.constraint_time: none — this is a CI fixture.
project.constraint_data: Only synthetic hostile strings; nothing real.
project.in_scope_list:
- Hostile values in every free-text answer field.
- Structured-file escaping (TOML & JSON) and prose passthrough.
project.pain_point: watches renderers choke on "ordinary" punctuation & symbols
project.key_benefit: proves hostile text lands verbatim as text, never as structure
project.key_differentiator: it is the test, not the product

positive_reference.ref: a repo with `ticks` & "quotes" in its tagline

negative_reference.ref: anything that switches on 'clever' escaping modes
